posts.title: Agentic RAG: retrieval as a repeated tool call, not a preprocessing step
posts.excerpt: Modern RAG best practice treats retrieval as something the agent can invoke multiple times during a task rather than once before generation. Combining dense vector search with sparse BM25 retrieval…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Modern RAG best practice treats retrieval as something the agent can invoke multiple times during a task rather than once before generation. Combining dense vector search with sparse BM25 retrieval and re-ranking improves precision significantly. Warning: multi-step reflection loops consume 3–10x more tokens than classic RAG — only worth it for complex reasoning workloads.

---
*Source: DigitalApplied*

posts.title: Fix agent failure modes systematically, not through prompt tweaks
posts.excerpt: When an agent makes a mistake, change the surrounding system (rules, tools, constraints, feedback loops) so that mistake structurally cannot recur — not just the prompt. Prompt patching compounds;…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
When an agent makes a mistake, change the surrounding system (rules, tools, constraints, feedback loops) so that mistake structurally cannot recur — not just the prompt. Prompt patching compounds; system-level fixes scale.

---
*Source: Sourcegraph / NxCode*

posts.title: Design tool interfaces with agents in mind
posts.excerpt: Agents perform better when tools expose clear filters, structured results, and easily parseable formats. If your team is building internal tools that agents will call, treat agent-readability as a…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Agents perform better when tools expose clear filters, structured results, and easily parseable formats. If your team is building internal tools that agents will call, treat agent-readability as a first-class design requirement alongside human usability.

---
*Source: Redis / Monday.com*

posts.title: KPMG deploys Claude to 276,000 employees across 138 countries

posts.content:
One of the largest enterprise AI rollouts to date, KPMG is using Claude at scale across its global workforce for advisory and consulting work. Signals accelerating mainstream enterprise adoption of frontier AI models.

*Source: BuildFastWithAI*

posts.title: OpenAI launches $4B consulting subsidiary "DeployCo
posts.excerpt: OpenAI officially launched a majority-owned standalone consulting entity backed by $4B from 19 investment firms and consultancies. They simultaneously acquired applied AI firm Tomoro (150…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
OpenAI officially launched a majority-owned standalone consulting entity backed by $4B from 19 investment firms and consultancies. They simultaneously acquired applied AI firm Tomoro (150 forward-deployed engineers). A significant strategic shift as OpenAI moves from model provider to full implementation partner.

---
*Source: BuildFastWithAI*

posts.title: Cohere acquires Aleph Alpha, creating a $20B transatlantic sovereign AI challenger
posts.excerpt: The deal merges two of the most prominent "non-US" AI companies into a single entity, positioning them as the go-to option for European and Canadian sovereign AI deployments. Major implications for…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
The deal merges two of the most prominent "non-US" AI companies into a single entity, positioning them as the go-to option for European and Canadian sovereign AI deployments. Major implications for regulated industries and government contracts globally.

---
*Source: AI Tools Recap*

posts.title: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni — open omni-modal model at 30B parameters
posts.excerpt: NVIDIA's new model unifies vision, audio, and language in a single 30B-parameter mixture-of-experts architecture. Open weights and omni-modal capability in a relatively compact model could be…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
NVIDIA's new model unifies vision, audio, and language in a single 30B-parameter mixture-of-experts architecture. Open weights and omni-modal capability in a relatively compact model could be significant for edge and on-device use cases.

---
*Source: LLM Stats*

posts.title: Google ships Gemini 3.5 and Gemini 3.5 Flash
posts.excerpt: Major Gemini model updates announced at Google I/O (May 19) are rolling out, improving performance, speed, and accessibility across Google AI services. Flash variant targets cost-sensitive,…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Major Gemini model updates announced at Google I/O (May 19) are rolling out, improving performance, speed, and accessibility across Google AI services. Flash variant targets cost-sensitive, high-throughput API workloads.

---
*Source: TechTimes*

posts.title: Anthropic launches Project Glasswing — AI-powered zero-day vulnerability research
posts.excerpt: Anthropic gave select organizations access to Claude Mythos Preview, a specialized security model that reportedly identified thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across major OSes and browsers in…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Anthropic gave select organizations access to *Claude Mythos Preview*, a specialized security model that reportedly identified thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across major OSes and browsers in weeks of internal testing. A significant signal of frontier AI moving into offensive security research.

---
*Source: crescendo.ai*

posts.title: AMD Venice enters production on TSMC 2nm
posts.excerpt: AMD kicked off manufacturing of its 6th-gen EPYC "Venice" server CPUs on TSMC's 2nm node — the first high-performance computing product at this process generation. Positions AMD as a credible…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
AMD kicked off manufacturing of its 6th-gen EPYC "Venice" server CPUs on TSMC's 2nm node — the first high-performance computing product at this process generation. Positions AMD as a credible challenger to NVIDIA's AI compute dominance as the node war intensifies.

---
*Source: crescendo.ai*
